sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a litter, such as one is carried in gently and comfortably; like the Germ. Sänfte, from the adj. fanft (see the root צָבַב . Pl. צַבִּים Isa 66:20. LXX. λαμπήνη. Num 7:3, עֶגְלֹת־צָב “litter-wagons;” i.e. like litters, commodious like litters. LXX. ἅμαξαι λαμπηνικαί.

(2) a species of lizard (Arab. ضَبُّ), so called from its slow motion, see the root, Lev 11:29. Bochart, Hieroz., i. p. 1044-63.
